articles.full_text:
Heatwave Disrupts Life in Odisha; Bhubaneswar Records 41°C by Afternoon
Intense heatwave conditions continue to grip Odisha, severely affecting daily life across several regions. The state capital, Bhubaneswar, recorded a high of 41°C by 2:30 PM on Wednesday. According to the Regional Meteorological Centre, Jharsuguda was the hottest in the state at 44.4°C, followed by Sambalpur at 43.2°C and Hirakud at 43°C.
Other regions also witnessed soaring temperatures, with Rourkela recording 41°C and Keonjhar at 40.2°C. Coastal areas experienced relatively lower temperatures, including Chandbali at 39.6°C and Balasore at 37.6°C.
Schools Closed Amid Rising Heat
With temperatures soaring, district administrations have announced temporary closure of schools in several regions. In Sundargarh, all schools from Class I to XII, including private institutions, will remain closed from April 23 to 27. Similarly, schools in Kendrapara and Koraput will remain shut from April 23 to 25, while Sambalpur schools will be closed from April 23 to 27.
Census Work Suspended During Peak Hours
The state’s Revenue and Disaster Management Department has also suspended census-related fieldwork during peak heat hours. Activities will remain halted between 11 AM and 3 PM to ensure the safety of staff amid extreme weather conditions.

articles.title: Odisha Is Boiling! Jharsuguda Hits 41.4°C By 11:30 AM; Severe Heatwave Alert In State! - Kanak News Odisha
articles.full_text:
Odisha is literally baking today! Even before the clock struck 12, temperatures have already crossed the dangerous 41-degree mark, making it one of the hottest mornings of the season.
The summer heat in Odisha has become unbearable right from the start of the day. According to the latest reports, Jharsuguda has recorded a staggering 41.4°C by 11:30 AM. Western Odisha is the worst hit with Sambalpur, Hirakud, and Rourkela recording extreme temperatures, while coastal areas like Bhubaneswar and Balasore are also sizzling.
